Definition: Calluna vulgaris genome assembly, chromosome: 7
Description: Calluna vulgaris genome assembly, chromosome: 7
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; eudicotyledons; Gunneridae; Pentapetalae; asterids; Ericales; Ericaceae; Ericoideae; Ericeae; Calluna
Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly ddCalVul5.1 of the common heather, Calluna vulgaris. The sample was provided by Thomas Marcussen, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). ddCalVul5.1 is based on 46x PacBio HiFi data and 122x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Definition: Sitta europaea genome assembly, chromosome: 4
Description: Sitta europaea genome assembly, chromosome: 4
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Archelosauria; Archosauria; Dinosauria; Saurischia; Theropoda; Coelurosauria; Aves; Neognathae; Neoaves; Telluraves; Australaves; Passeriformes; Sittidae; Sitta
Assembly: This project provides the diploid genome assembly bSitEur1.1 of the Eurasian nuthatch, Sitta europaea. The sample was provided by Jan T. Lifjeld, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). bSitEur is based on 34x PacBio HiFi data and 88x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Definition: Diacrisia sannio genome assembly, chromosome: 13
Description: Diacrisia sannio genome assembly, chromosome: 13
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Noctuoidea; Erebidae; Arctiinae; Diacrisia
Assembly: The assembly ilDiaSann1.hap2.1 is based on 56x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4) and read coverage.
